{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|02.60|N|137|02|20.14|E}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=draft}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=draft}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=draft}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=draft}} and include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History, *New Nagoya City History: Archaeological Sources 1*, Nagoya City, 2008. * {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=draft}}, *Miharashidai Archaeological Museum Report "Miharashi" No. 257*, 2008. * Nagoya City Board of Education designation1 Division, *2020 Excavation Results Presentation for Nishi-Okute Kofun*, January 16, 2021. == Access == * {{ill|Ozone Station|ja|大曽根駅|12=draft}} ({{ill|JR Central Main Line|ja|中央本線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|ja|名古屋市営地下鉄|12=draft}} Meijo Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|12=draft}} (JR Central Main Line, {{ill|Aichi Loop Railway|ja|愛知環状鉄道|12=draft}} Aichi Loop Line): ** Alight at {{ill|Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|ja|東谷橋|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Guideway Bus Shidami Line|ja|ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=draft}} (Yutorito Line). == See Also == * {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nishi Okute Kofun}} * [http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kurashi/kyoiku/bunka/rekishinosato/nagoya00057527.html Nagoya City: Nishi-Okute Kofun (Living Intypeation)] * {{Wayback|url=http://blogs.yahoo.co.jp/area19192003/50929774.html |title=Aichi Limited History Report Large Text Version – Okute Kofun Excavation Photo Report No. 2 |date=*}} * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/2005-09-14 The Door to China and Six Dynasties, Part 4: Higashi-Okute Kofun and Nishi-Okute Kofun] {{Japanese-history-stub}} == Higashi Oohisa Kofun Initially, Higashi Ōkute and {{ill|Nishi Ōkute Kofun|ja|西大久手古墳|12=draft}} were thought to share the same axis and area, indicating a close re ... 5 KB (624 words) - 07:52, 15 January 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun == * {{ill|Nishi Oohisa Kofun|ja|西大久手古墳|12=draft}}

(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|02.60|N|137|02|20.14|E}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=draft|12=draft}} and include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|02.60|N|137|02|20.14|E}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}} and include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History, *New Nagoya City History: Archaeological Sources 1*, Nagoya City, 2008. * {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}, *Miharashidai Archaeological Museum Report "Miharashi" No. 257*, 2008. * Nagoya City Board of Education designation1 Division, *2020 Excavation Results Presentation for Nishi-Okute Kofun*, January 16, 2021. == Access == * {{ill|Ozone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} ({{ill|JR Central Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}}, {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|ja|名古屋市営地下鉄}} Meijo Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Central Main Line, {{ill|Aichi Loop Railway|ja|愛知環状鉄道}} Aichi Loop Line): ** Alight at {{ill|Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|ja|東谷橋}} on the {{ill|Guideway Bus Shidami Line|ja|ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorito Line). == See Also == * {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nishi Okute Kofun}} * [http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kurashi/kyoiku/bunka/rekishinosato/nagoya00057527.html Nagoya City: Nishi-Okute Kofun (Living Intypeation)] * {{Wayback|url=http://blogs.yahoo.co.jp/area19192003/50929774.html |title=Aichi Limited History Report Large Text Version – Okute Kofun Excavation Photo Report No. 2 |date=*}} * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/2005-09-14 The Door to China and Six Dynasties, Part 4: Higashi-Okute Kofun and Nishi-Okute Kofun] {{Japanese-history-stub}} == Higashi Oohisa Kofun Initially, Higashi Ōkute and {{ill|Nishi Ōkute Kofun|ja|西大久手古墳}} were thought to share the same axis and area, indicating a close re ... 5 KB (624 words) - revision.comment: 19 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:西大久手古墳 up to rev 92909719
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=西大久手古墳 |画像=[[画像:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|西大久手古墳（2019年（令和元年）5月）]] |画像キャプション =西大久手古墳
（2019年（令和元年）5月） |所在地=[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]上志段味字大久手下 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|15|02.60|N|137|02|20.14|E|}} |形状=帆立貝型前方後円墳 |規模=墳丘長 約39m、全長 約59m（周濠含む） 、後円墳径約27m、高さ不明 |築造年代=5世紀中頃 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=須恵器、埴輪 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''西大久手古墳'''（にしおおくてこふん）は[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]にある[[古墳]]。[[志段味古墳群]]のひとつ。 == 概要 == 大久手池の周辺に点在する大久手古墳群の[[前方後円墳]]。大久手2号墳と呼ばれる場合もある。最初に調査が行なわれたのは[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）・[[1983年]]（昭和58年）の愛知県教育委員会の依頼による[[名古屋大学]]の測量調査だが、それ以降[[2005年]]（[[平成]]17年）に名古屋市教育委員会が[[発掘調査]]をおこなうまで公式な調査はほとんどされなかった。 従来は隣接する[[東大久手古墳]]と主軸が同じであるとして双方の関連性が言われていたが、近年の調査で主軸がずれていることが判明したほか、前方部の特徴（後述）などから[[志段味大塚古墳]]との関連性が高い可能性が指摘されている。また、[[2008年]]（平成20年）の発掘調査で[[東海地方]]以東では最古級（[[5世紀]]中頃）のものとされる人物埴輪が出土した。同時期の人物埴輪は近畿地方の[[天皇陵]]などで見つかっていることから、この地方の勢力が[[ヤマト王権]]と深い繋がりを持っていた事が考えられるという。 == 規模と構造 == 前方部が短い帆立貝型前方後円墳で、墳丘長は約39メートル、周濠部分を含めると全長約59メートル、後円部の直径が約27メートルと推定される。年月を経る間に墳丘の削平が進んでおり、現存するのは表土から50センチメートル程度のため、前方部・後円部とも高さについては数値が算出されていない。 [[2005年]]（平成17年）の発掘調査で、前方部の傾斜角が下部で約12度、上部で約40度と変化している構造が確認された。同様の構造は近接する[[志段味大塚古墳]]でも確認されている。後円部では傾斜角の変化を確認できていないことから前方部の3面のみに設けられていた可能性が高い。 周濠は一重で逆台形の断面を持ち、現在の残存高から約1.1メートルの深さがある。外堤の上部に幅60センチメートル程度の平坦面があったと見られているが、後円部側では外堤も掘平されている。また、周濠底から[[葺石]]とみられる石材が見つかっているが墳丘部では葺石を確認できないため、後年の削平などの際にほとんど落下したと考えられている。 [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）度の発掘で南側に造り出しが発見されている。 [[2020年]]（令和2年）度の発掘で南側くびれ部から前方部に葺石が検出され、造り出しの辺りからは、高坏形器台の破片や円筒埴輪以外の埴輪の破片など、墳丘上に祭祀に使われたと思われる遺物が出土している。後円部の周壕部分より有頸有茎鏃の一種で短頸平根系柳葉式鉄鏃が見つかっている。 ファイル:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|西大久手古墳（左下）と周辺の古墳（1987年（昭和62年））{{国土航空写真}} ファイル:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|2008年当時の西大久手古墳（2008年（平成20年）7月） == 出土品 == * [[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]収蔵 ** [[須恵器]] ** [[円筒埴輪]] ** 馬型埴輪 ** 鶏型埴輪 ** 人物埴輪 ** 鉄鏃 == 参考文献 == * [[名古屋市教育委員会]] 『志段味大塚古墳・大久手古墳群 - 平成17年度大久手池周辺埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書』、2006年 * 名古屋市教育委員会 『大久手池周辺古墳群 - 平成18年度「歴史の里（仮称）」計画地内埋蔵文化財範囲確認調査』、2007年 * 新修名古屋市史資料編編集委員会『[[新修名古屋市史]] 資料編 考古1』、名古屋市、2008年 * [[名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]『名古屋市見晴台考古資料館報 みはらし No.257』、2008年 * 名古屋市教育委員会文化財保護室『令和2年度西大久手古墳発掘調査成果報告会』 2021年1月16日 == アクセス == * [[大曽根駅]]（[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央線 (名古屋地区)|中央本線]]、[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄瀬戸線|瀬戸線]]、[[名古屋市営地下鉄]][[名古屋市営地下鉄名城線|名城線]]） * [[高蔵寺駅]]（JR中央本線、[[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]]）より ** [[名古屋ガイドウェイバス]][[名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|ガイドウェイバス志段味線]]（[[ゆとりーとライン]]） 「東谷橋」バス停下車。 == 関連項目 == * [[志段味古墳群]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Nishi Okute Kofun}} * [http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kurashi/kyoiku/bunka/rekishinosato/nagoya00057527.html 名古屋市:西大久手古墳（暮らしの情報）] * {{Wayback|url=http://blogs.yahoo.co.jp/area19192003/50929774.html |title=愛知限定 歴史レポ 大文字版 - 大久手古墳発掘写真レポＮｏ２ |date=*}} * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/2005-09-14 中国と六朝の扉 その四、東大久手古墳・西大久手古墳] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にしおおくてこふん}} [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:志段味古墳群]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=西大久手古墳 |画像=[[画像:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|西大久手古墳（2019年（令和元年）5月）]] |画像キャプション =西大久手古墳
（2019年（令和元年）5月） |所在地=[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]上志段味字大久手下 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|15|02.60|N|137|02|20.14|E|}} |形状=帆立貝型前方後円墳 |規模=墳丘長 約39m、全長 約59m（周濠含む） 、後円墳径約27m、高さ不明 |築造年代=5世紀中頃 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=須恵器、埴輪 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''西大久手古墳'''（にしおおくてこふん）は[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[守山区]]にある[[古墳]]。[[志段味古墳群]]のひとつ。 == 概要 == 大久手池の周辺に点在する大久手古墳群の[[前方後円墳]]。大久手2号墳と呼ばれる場合もある。最初に調査が行なわれたのは[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）・[[1983年]]（昭和58年）の愛知県教育委員会の依頼による[[名古屋大学]]の測量調査だが、それ以降[[2005年]]（[[平成]]17年）に名古屋市教育委員会が[[発掘調査]]をおこなうまで公式な調査はほとんどされなかった。 従来は隣接する[[東大久手古墳]]と主軸が同じであるとして双方の関連性が言われていたが、近年の調査で主軸がずれていることが判明したほか、前方部の特徴（後述）などから[[志段味大塚古墳]]との関連性が高い可能性が指摘されている。また、[[2008年]]（平成20年）の発掘調査で[[東海地方]]以東では最古級（[[5世紀]]中頃）のものとされる人物埴輪が出土した。同時期の人物埴輪は近畿地方の[[天皇陵]]などで見つかっていることから、この地方の勢力が[[ヤマト王権]]と深い繋がりを持っていた事が考えられるという。 == 規模と構造 == 前方部が短い帆立貝型前方後円墳で、墳丘長は約39メートル、周濠部分を含めると全長約59メートル、後円部の直径が約27メートルと推定される。年月を経る間に墳丘の削平が進んでおり、現存するのは表土から50センチメートル程度のため、前方部・後円部とも高さについては数値が算出されていない。 [[2005年]]（平成17年）の発掘調査で、前方部の傾斜角が下部で約12度、上部で約40度と変化している構造が確認された。同様の構造は近接する[[志段味大塚古墳]]でも確認されている。後円部では傾斜角の変化を確認できていないことから前方部の3面のみに設けられていた可能性が高い。 周濠は一重で逆台形の断面を持ち、現在の残存高から約1.1メートルの深さがある。外堤の上部に幅60センチメートル程度の平坦面があったと見られているが、後円部側では外堤も掘平されている。また、周濠底から[[葺石]]とみられる石材が見つかっているが墳丘部では葺石を確認できないため、後年の削平などの際にほとんど落下したと考えられている。 [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）度の発掘で南側に造り出しが発見されている。 [[2020年]]（令和2年）度の発掘で南側くびれ部から前方部に葺石が検出され、造り出しの辺りからは、高坏形器台の破片や円筒埴輪以外の埴輪の破片など、墳丘上に祭祀に使われたと思われる遺物が出土している。後円部の周壕部分より有頸有茎鏃の一種で短頸平根系柳葉式鉄鏃が見つかっている。 ファイル:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|西大久手古墳（左下）と周辺の古墳（1987年（昭和62年））{{国土航空写真}} ファイル:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|2008年当時の西大久手古墳（2008年（平成20年）7月） == 出土品 == * [[見晴台遺跡#名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]収蔵 ** [[須恵器]] ** [[円筒埴輪]] ** 馬型埴輪 ** 鶏型埴輪 ** 人物埴輪 ** 鉄鏃 == 参考文献 == * [[名古屋市教育委員会]] 『志段味大塚古墳・大久手古墳群 - 平成17年度大久手池周辺埋蔵文化財発掘調査報告書』、2006年 * 名古屋市教育委員会 『大久手池周辺古墳群 - 平成18年度「歴史の里（仮称）」計画地内埋蔵文化財範囲確認調査』、2007年 * 新修名古屋市史資料編編集委員会『[[新修名古屋市史]] 資料編 考古1』、名古屋市、2008年 * [[名古屋市見晴台考古資料館]]『名古屋市見晴台考古資料館報 みはらし No.257』、2008年 * 名古屋市教育委員会文化財保護室『令和2年度西大久手古墳発掘調査成果報告会』 2021年1月16日 == アクセス == * [[大曽根駅]]（[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央線 (名古屋地区)|中央本線]]、[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄瀬戸線|瀬戸線]]、[[名古屋市営地下鉄]][[名古屋市営地下鉄名城線|名城線]]） * [[高蔵寺駅]]（JR中央本線、[[愛知環状鉄道]]・[[愛知環状鉄道線]]）より ** [[名古屋ガイドウェイバス]][[名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|ガイドウェイバス志段味線]]（[[ゆとりーとライン]]） 「東谷橋」バス停下車。 == 関連項目 == * [[志段味古墳群]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Nishi Okute Kofun}} * [http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kurashi/kyoiku/bunka/rekishinosato/nagoya00057527.html 名古屋市:西大久手古墳（暮らしの情報）] * {{Wayback|url=http://blogs.yahoo.co.jp/area19192003/50929774.html |title=愛知限定 歴史レポ 大文字版 - 大久手古墳発掘写真レポＮｏ２ |date=*}} * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/2005-09-14 中国と六朝の扉 その四、東大久手古墳・西大久手古墳] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にしおおくてこふん}} [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:守山区の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:志段味古墳群]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|02.60|N|137|02|20.14|E}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}} and include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History, *New Nagoya City History: Archaeological Sources 1*, Nagoya City, 2008. * {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館}}, *Miharashidai Archaeological Museum Report "Miharashi" No. 257*, 2008. * Nagoya City Board of Education designation1 Division, *2020 Excavation Results Presentation for Nishi-Okute Kofun*, January 16, 2021. == Access == * {{ill|Ozone Station|ja|大曽根駅}} ({{ill|JR Central Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線}}, {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|ja|名古屋市営地下鉄}} Meijo Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅}} (JR Central Main Line, {{ill|Aichi Loop Railway|ja|愛知環状鉄道}} Aichi Loop Line): ** Alight at {{ill|Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|ja|東谷橋}} on the {{ill|Guideway Bus Shidami Line|ja|ガイドウェイバス志段味線}} (Yutorito Line). == See Also == * {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nishi Okute Kofun}} * [http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kurashi/kyoiku/bunka/rekishinosato/nagoya00057527.html Nagoya City: Nishi-Okute Kofun (Living Intypeation)] * {{Wayback|url=http://blogs.yahoo.co.jp/area19192003/50929774.html |title=Aichi Limited History Report Large Text Version – Okute Kofun Excavation Photo Report No. 2 |date=*}} * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/2005-09-14 The Door to China and Six Dynasties, Part 4: Higashi-Okute Kofun and Nishi-Okute Kofun] {{Japanese-history-stub}} == Higashi Oohisa Kofun Initially, Higashi Ōkute and {{ill|Nishi Ōkute Kofun|ja|西大久手古墳}} were thought to share the same axis and area, indicating a close re ... 5 KB (624 words) - This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|02.60|N|137|02|20.14|E}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|12=draft}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=draft}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=draft}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=draft}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=draft}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=draft}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|qq=draft}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|qq=draft}} and include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}} * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History, *New Nagoya City History: Archaeological Sources 1*, Nagoya City, 2008. * {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|qq=draft}}, *Miharashidai Archaeological Museum Report "Miharashi" No. 257*, 2008. * Nagoya City Board of Education designation1 Division, *2020 Excavation Results Presentation for Nishi-Okute Kofun*, January 16, 2021. == Access == * {{ill|Ozone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft}} ({{ill|JR Central Main Line|ja|中央本線|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|ja|名古屋市営地下鉄|qq=draft}} Meijo Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft}} (JR Central Main Line, {{ill|Aichi Loop Railway|ja|愛知環状鉄道|qq=draft}} Aichi Loop Line): ** Alight at {{ill|Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|ja|東谷橋|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Guideway Bus Shidami Line|ja|ガイドウェイバス志段味線|qq=draft}} (Yutorito Line). == See Also == * {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nishi Okute Kofun}} * [http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kurashi/kyoiku/bunka/rekishinosato/nagoya00057527.html Nagoya City: Nishi-Okute Kofun (Living Intypeation)] * {{Wayback|url=http://blogs.yahoo.co.jp/area19192003/50929774.html |title=Aichi Limited History Report Large Text Version – Okute Kofun Excavation Photo Report No. 2 |date=*}} * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/2005-09-14 The Door to China and Six Dynasties, Part 4: Higashi-Okute Kofun and Nishi-Okute Kofun] {{Japanese-history-stub}} == Higashi Oohisa Kofun Initially, Higashi Ōkute and {{ill|Nishi Ōkute Kofun|ja|西大久手古墳|qq=draft}} were thought to share the same axis and area, indicating a close re ... 5 KB (624 words) - Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|02.60|N|137|02|20.14|E}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Nishi Oohisa Kofun]] → [[Nishi Ōkute Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|02.60|N|137|02|20.14|E}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama Ward|ja|守山区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagoya|ja|名古屋市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|qq=}} and include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History, *New Nagoya City History: Archaeological Sources 1*, Nagoya City, 2008. * {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|qq=}}, *Miharashidai Archaeological Museum Report "Miharashi" No. 257*, 2008. * Nagoya City Board of Education designation1 Division, *2020 Excavation Results Presentation for Nishi-Okute Kofun*, January 16, 2021. == Access == * {{ill|Ozone Station|ja|大曽根駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozone Station|qq=}} ({{ill|JR Central Main Line|ja|中央本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central Main Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|ja|名鉄瀬戸線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Seto Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|ja|名古屋市営地下鉄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Municipal Subway|qq=}} Meijo Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|ja|高蔵寺駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzōji Station|qq=}} (JR Central Main Line, {{ill|Aichi Loop Railway|ja|愛知環状鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Loop Railway|qq=}} Aichi Loop Line): ** Alight at {{ill|Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|ja|東谷橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|qq=}} on the {{ill|Guideway Bus Shidami Line|ja|ガイドウェイバス志段味線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guideway Bus Shidami Line|qq=}} (Yutorito Line). == See Also == * {{ill|Shidami Kofun Cluster|ja|志段味古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} == ... 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January categorry:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西大久手古墳|version=92909719|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama Ward]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Nishi Ōkute Kofun]] [[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A4%A7%E4%B9%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|02.60|N|137|02|20.14|E}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Cluster]]. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|qq=}} and include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History, *New Nagoya City History: Archaeological Sources 1*, Nagoya City, 2008. * {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|qq=}}, *Miharashidai Archaeological Museum Report "Miharashi" No. 257*, 2008. * Nagoya City Board of Education designation1 Division, *2020 Excavation Results Presentation for Nishi-Okute Kofun*, January 16, 2021. == Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ozone Station]] ([[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Central Main Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Seto Line|Meitetsu Seto Line]], [[:en:Nagoya Municipal Subway|Nagoya Municipal Subway]] Meijo Line) * [[:en:Kōzōji Station|Kōzōji Station]] (JR Central Main Line, {{ill|Aichi Loop Railway|ja|愛知環状鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Loop Railway|qq=}} Aichi Loop Line): ** Alight at {{ill|Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|ja|東谷橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|qq=}} on the {{ill|Guideway Bus Shidami Line|ja|ガイドウェイバス志段味線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guideway Bus Shidami Line|qq=}} (Yutorito Line). == See Also == * [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Cluster]] * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nishi Okute Kofun}} * [http://www.city.nagoya.jp/kurashi/kyoiku/bunka/rekishinosato/nagoya00057527.html Nagoya City: Nishi-Okute Kofun (Living Intypeation)] * {{Wayback|url=http://blogs.yahoo.co.jp/area19192003/50929774.html |title=Aichi Limited History Report Large Text Version – Okute Kofun Excavation Photo Report No. 2 |date=*}} * [https://fkoryuliuchao.blog.ss-blog.jp/2005-09-14 The Door to China and Six Dynasties, Part 4: Higashi-Okute Kofun and Nishi-Okute Kofun] {{Japanese-history-stub}} == Higashi Oohisa Kofun Initially, Higashi Ōkute and {{ill|Nishi Ōkute Kofun|ja|西大久手古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi Ōkute Kofun|qq=}} were thought to share the same axis and area, indicating a close re ... 5 KB (624 words) - 07:52, 15 January 2025 Scallop-shaped Kofun == * {{ill|Nishi Ōkute Kofun|lt=Nishi Ōkute Kofun|lt=Nishi Oohisa Kofun|ja|西大久手古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi Oohisa Kofun|qq=}}
... 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January categorry:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|西大久手古墳|version=92909719|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Moriyama Ward]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Shidami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Nishi Ōkute Kofun]] [[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A4%A7%E4%B9%85%E6%89%8B%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|qq=}} and include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History, *New Nagoya City History: Archaeological Sources 1*, Nagoya City, 2008. * {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35|15|02.60|N|137|02|20.14|E}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Cluster]]. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|qq=}} and include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue
