(May 2019) | location = [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.250722|N|137.038928|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Cluster]]. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}} and include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City [[sn:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11627539]] Wikidata name: Nishi Okute Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.250722|N|137.038928|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Cluster]]. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}} and include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * [[commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11627539]] Wikidata name: Nishi Okute Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.250722|N|137.038928|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Cluster]]. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}} and include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History, *New Nagoya City History: Archaeological Sources 1*, Nagoya City, 2008. * {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}, *Miharashidai Archaeological Museum Report "Miharashi" No. 257*, 2008. * Nagoya City Board of Education designation1 Division, *2020 Excavation Results Presentation for Nishi-Okute Kofun*, January 16, 2021. == Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ozone Station]] ([[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Central Main Line]], [[commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11627539]] Wikidata name: Nishi Okute Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.250722|N|137.038928|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Cluster]]. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}} and include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History, *New Nagoya City History: Archaeological Sources 1*, Nagoya City, 2008. * {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}, *Miharashidai Archaeological Museum Report "Miharashi" No. 257*, 2008. * Nagoya City Board of Education designation1 Division, *2020 Excavation Results Presentation for Nishi-Okute Kofun*, January 16, 2021. == Access == * [[:en:Ōzone Station|Ozone Station]] ([[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Central Main Line]], Railway|ja|愛知環状鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Loop Railway|1=Aichi Loop Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494075}} Aichi Loop Line): ** Alight at {{ill|Higashiya Bridge Bus Shidami Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|1=Guideway Bus Shidami Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q850582}} (Yutorito Line). == See Also == * [[:en:Shidami It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Cluster]]. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. [[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11627539]] Wikidata name: Nishi Okute Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.250722|N|137.038928|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|Moriyama Ward]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the [[:en:Shidami Kofun group|Shidami Kofun Cluster]]. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}} and include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11627539]] Wikidata name: Nishi Okute Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.250722|N|137.038928|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}} and include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History, *New Nagoya City History: Archaeological Sources 1*, Nagoya City, 2008. * {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}, *Miharashidai Archaeological Museum Report "Miharashi" No. 257*, 2008. * Nagoya City Board of Education designation1 Division, *2020 Excavation Results Presentation for Nishi-Okute Kofun*, January 16, 2021. == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|en|Ōzone Station|WD=Q872075|lt=Ozone Station}} ({{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=JR Central Main Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|en|Meitetsu Seto Line|WD=Q6810422|lt=Meitetsu Seto Line}}, {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|en|Nagoya Municipal Subway|WD=Q1144874|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway}} Meijo Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|en|Kōzōji Station|WD=Q5359256|lt=Kōzōji Station}} (JR Central Main Line, {{ill|Aichi Loop Railway|ja|愛知環状鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Loop Railway|1=Aichi Loop Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494075}} Aichi Loop Line): ** Alight at {{ill|Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|ja|東谷橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the {{ill|Guideway Bus Shidami Line|ja|ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guideway Bus Shidami Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|1=Guideway Bus Shidami Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q850582}} (Yutorito Line). == See Also == * {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed [[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.250722|N|137.038928|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}} and include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}} and include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute [[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.250722|N|137.038928|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai [[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.250722|N|137.038928|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Nishi Okute Kofun]][[ja:西大久手古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nishi-Okute Kofun | image = [[File:Nishi-Okute Kofun, Kamishidami Moriyama Ward Nagoya 2019.jpg|250px|Nishi-Okute Kofun (May 2019)]] | caption = Nishi-Okute Kofun
(May 2019) | location = {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture, Kamishidami, Oogute-shimo | coordinates = {{Coord|35.250722|N|137.038928|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Scallop-shaped kofun with a short anterior mound | area = Mound length: approx. 39 m; total length (including moat): approx. 59 m; diameter of posterior mound: approx. 27 m; height: unknown | built = Mid-5th century | burial = | Interred Individual = | burial goods = Sue pottery, haniwa | designation1 = | notes = }} '''Nishi-Okute Kofun''' (西大久手古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|WD=Q1155291|lt=Moriyama Ward|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}}. == Overview == The Nishi-Okute Kofun is a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} situated near the Okute Pond and is part of the {{ill|Okute Kofun Cluster|ja|大久手古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okute Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is also referred to as "Okute No. 2 Kofun." Initial surveys were conducted in 1982 and 1983 by Nagoya University at the request of the Aichi Prefecture Board of Education. However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound. File:Otsuka and Okute kofun group.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun (lower left) and nearby tombs (1987) File:Nishi Okute kofun02.jpg|Nishi-Okute Kofun in July 2008 == burial goods == Items excavated from the kofun are housed at the {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}} and include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} * Cylindrical haniwa * Horse-shaped haniwa * Chicken-shaped haniwa * Human-shaped haniwa * Iron arrowheads == References == * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Excavation Report of Shidami-Otsuka Kofun and Okute Kofun Cluster - FY 2005 Excavations Around Okute Pond*, 2006. * Nagoya City Board of Education, *Ancient Tombs Around Okute Pond - FY 2006 Investigation of Historic Site (Provisional name) Area*, 2007. * Editorial Committee for the New Nagoya City History, *New Nagoya City History: Archaeological Sources 1*, Nagoya City, 2008. * {{ill|Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja|名古屋市見晴台考古資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miharashidai Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 見晴台遺跡|1=Miharashidai Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630197}}, *Miharashidai Archaeological Museum Report "Miharashi" No. 257*, 2008. * Nagoya City Board of Education designation1 Division, *2020 Excavation Results Presentation for Nishi-Okute Kofun*, January 16, 2021. == Access == * {{ill|Ōzone Station|en|Ōzone Station|WD=Q872075|lt=Ozone Station|1=Ōzone Station}} ({{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=JR Central Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Seto Line|en|Meitetsu Seto Line|WD=Q6810422|lt=Meitetsu Seto Line|1=Meitetsu Seto Line}}, {{ill|Nagoya Municipal Subway|en|Nagoya Municipal Subway|WD=Q1144874|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}} Meijo Line) * {{ill|Kōzōji Station|en|Kōzōji Station|WD=Q5359256|lt=Kōzōji Station|1=Kōzōji Station}} (JR Central Main Line, {{ill|Aichi Loop Railway|ja|愛知環状鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Loop Railway|1=Aichi Loop Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494075}} Aichi Loop Line): ** Alight at {{ill|Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|ja|東谷橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiya Bridge Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the {{ill|Guideway Bus Shidami Line|ja|ガイドウェイバス志段味線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guideway Bus Shidami Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名古屋ガイドウェイバスガイドウェイバス志段味線|1=Guideway Bus Shidami Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q850582}} (Yutorito Line). == See Also == * {{ill|Shidami Kofun group|en|Shidami Kofun group|WD=Q11491064|lt=Shidami Kofun Cluster|1=Shidami Kofun group}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of However, official investigations were scarce until 2005, when the Nagoya City Board of Education began excavations. Historically, the kofun was believed to share its primary axis with the neighboring {{ill|Higashi-Okute Kofun|ja|東大久手古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Okute Kofun|qq=}}, but recent surveys revealed a slight misalignment. Its anterior section bears structural similarities to the {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, suggesting a closer connection. During the 2008 excavation, one of the oldest human-shaped haniwa in the Tokai region, dated to the mid-5th century, was discovered. Similar haniwa have been found in keyhole-shaped tombs associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} in the Kinki region, suggesting strong ties between local rulers and the Yamato political power. == map_size and burial == The Nishi-Okute Kofun features a short anterior mound characteristic of scallop-shaped kofun. The mound measures approximately 39 meters in length, with a total length of about 59 meters when including the surrounding moat. The posterior mound's diameter is estimated at 27 meters, but the height of both sections remains unknown due to significant erosion. Currently, the mound rises only about 50 centimeters above the surface. Excavations in 2005 revealed that the anterior mound's slope angle transitions from approximately 12 degrees at its base to 40 degrees near the top, a feature also seen in the nearby {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|ja|志段味大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami-Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}. The posterior mound does not exhibit such a transition, suggesting that the steep slopes were confined to the three visible sides of the anterior section. The moat surrounding the mound is single-layered with an inverted trapezoid cross-section, reaching a depth of about 1.1 meters. A flat surface about 60 centimeters wide was identified along the top of the outer embankment, although this feature has been eroded near the posterior mound. Excavated materials, such as stones believed to be paving stones, were found on the moat bottom, but no evidence of paving stones remains on the mound itself, likely due to erosion over time. In 2019, additional excavations uncovered a protrusion on the southern side of the mound. In 2020, paving stones were found extending from the southern constriction to the anterior mound. Additionally, fragments of high-footed pedestal pottery, cylindrical haniwa, and other ceremonial items used for rituals on the mound were recovered. A type of iron arrowhead (柳葉式鉄鏃) was discovered in the moat surrounding the posterior mound.
